title: container_t isn't allowed to access container_var_lib_t
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: # Uncomment only one, leave it on its own line:

# **kind bug**

[//]: # **kind feature**

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Deploy some container with *docker* and some volumes (let docker create the host directories)

2. Migrate the container to Podman, with that same directory

3. Try to read/write in the volume from podman

**Describe the results you received:**
We get SELinux access denied, for instance:
```
type=AVC msg=audit(1541628356.826:5369): avc: denied { read } for pid=452886 comm="python" name="rabbitmq-env.conf" dev="dm-0" ino=100801360 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c41,c44 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:container_var_lib_t:s0 tclass=file
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
We should be able to access the data in the same way we could with docker


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
It would be cool to be able to access the data without any "chcon" - especially if there are many directory levels and many files.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.10.2-dev
Go Version:    go1.10.2
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.5-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.10.1.3-4.gitee513cc.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0, commit: 01d038c5b51fddd21be5210091707dd15bb830e0-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"centos"'
    version: "7"
  MemFree: 6690496512
  MemTotal: 25111056384
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-54.dev.git2abd837.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 12683571200
  SwapTotal: 12683571200
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 10
  hostname: undercloud.localdomain
  kernel: 3.10.0-862.14.4.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 20h 9m 1.95s (Approximately 0.83 days)
insecure registries:
  registries:
  - 192.168.24.1:8787
  - 192.168.24.3:8787
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 18
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 22
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
This issue is also described on Launchpad: https://bugs.launchpad.net/tripleo/+bug/1802203

comments.body: @rhatdan hello! if you have any thoughts about this issue.. :) Thanks!

Looks like you have a mislabeled overlay directory in /var/lib/containers
restorecon -R -v /varlib/containers

See if it changes the labels under overlay to container_share_t?
@rhatdan The original reason for this was to migrate a system from Docker to Podman - Docker volumes were labelled as container_var_lib_t

The default label for content in /var/lib/docker and /var/lib/containers is container_var_lib_t, This is not r/w/x by containers whether they are running under docker or under podman,cri-o, buildah ...

content under /varlib/containers/overlay* and /var/lib/docker/overlay* is labeled container_share_ by default.
```
 grep overlay /etc/selinux/targeted/contexts/files/file_contexts
/var/lib/docker/overlay(/.*)?	system_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0
/var/lib/docker/overlay2(/.*)?	system_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0
/var/lib/containers/overlay(/.*)?	system_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0
/var/lib/containers/overlay2(/.*)?	system_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0
/var/lib/docker-latest/overlay(/.*)?	system_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0
/var/lib/docker-latest/overlay2(/.*)?	system_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay(/.*)?	system_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay2(/.*)?	system_u:object_r:container_share_t:s0
```

container_share_t is r/x by containers.  Not writeable.  

The only label that is writeable by containers is container_file_t, which is used when the overlay mount is created for the upper directory of an image. Or is used for content mounted from devicemapper.  If you volume mount in a directory into  a container and add a :z or :Z the content is relabeled to container_file_t.

So a failure to r/w/x content labeled container_var_lib_t is expected.  Which is why I expect that this is either a volume mounted in from /var/lib/container or /var/lib/docker or a mislabeled content undev /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay

Hello,
Well, unfortunately, the tree isn't the one you'd expect. For instance the log pointed in the LP issue linked in the current issue is showing this file:
"IOError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: u'/var/lib/kolla/config_files/src/etc/rabbitmq/rabbitmq-env.conf'",

We're not under any docker/podman dedicated directory, and the SELinux type wasn't set until lately, because docker daemon was running with the selinux separation deactivated.
In most of the cases, the /var/lib/kolla directory is already existing, but the config_files not, and was created by the first docker container with the bind mount. It then run containers with that bind-mount, running puppet processes in order to generate the configurations, and then starts the "productive" container.

The current situation is:
- /var/lib/kolla/config_files was created under docker, without selinux separation
- that same directory (and its content) is mounted in various docker container
- and we want to migrate from docker to podman, using those very same, existing locations with generated configurations as the same bind-mount.

This apparently isn't possible for some reason, and we really suspect docker creates the host non-existing volumes with the container_var_lib_t, and this was NOT an issue before because of the deactivation of some parts of SELinux (namely, the labels, although this should not impact the setype, only the sXX)...

Of course we can add a migration step setting the setype to container_file_t, but that might be an issue for some locations with either deep file tree, or many files, as it might take very long for the ```chcon -R```

A restorcon will probably not work as it's in a tree not managed by any package, and at least NOT in docker/podman standard locations (named volumes weren't used, and as they aren't supported in podman, it's a good thing).

If you want the content to be shared read/only  with containers run via podman and docker, then you could label it container_share_t.

semanage fcontext -a -t container_share_t "/var/lib/kolla(/.*)?"
restorecon -R -v /var/lib/kolla

Hmm we have some read-write as well in that location, hence container_file_t might be best.

If I understand correctly, a chcon (or fcontext) is mandatory in any cases, unless we push some new policy from openstack-selinux or the like (and that modification will probably be rejected right?).

Guess we can close this request as "not a bug".
Well you can also volume mount with the ":z" or ":Z"
